title: Rei is Shinjis sister not
wikitext:
[]: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Nov 28, 2019 4:02 pm Where does that come from!? Yesterday I was having lunch and I overheard a girl talking about anime with her companions... When she mentioned Evangelion I started to pay attention too, dare I say, to see how much she understood the series (yeah, I put myself on top of a Eva high ground! Judge if you want ) Thing is, it was all going well: Evan-jelly-on, the robots that defend the humanity against entities named Angels and that can only be piloted by motherless children. I'll take she was trying to be spoiler-free by saying that instead of specifying that the pilot's mother's soul was supposed to be in the Eva. But then she hit the discordant note: when describing the pilots she said -literally and without possibility of ambiguity- that Rei is Shinji's sister... I know Evangelion is not by any means straightforward, but unlike the rather obscure fact that Rei's soul was Lilith's, we never receive info that suggests Rei is nothing other than a clone of Yui (or at least 90% Yui and a bit of Lilith). Thing is, this is not the first time I've heard this misconception: some years ago I heard another person telling the exact same thing when explaining Eva. Have you heard this before? What do you suppose causes it?

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Akko » Thu Nov 28, 2019 5:21 pm A clone shares genetics with the original just like a kid shares genetics with the mother, but that doesn't mean clone = kid because if Rei was Shinji's sister, she would also share genetics with Gendo and that's pretty unlikely if I do say so myself

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Nov 28, 2019 6:08 pm To put it another way, Rei being related to only one of Shinji's parents makes her his half sister. Alternatively, being a clone of his mother, she is in a sense his mother. And indeed the series plays with that by having Shinji remark that she is "like a mother".

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Fri Nov 29, 2019 1:25 am pwhodges wrote: To put it another way, Rei being related to only one of Shinji's parents makes her his half sister. And indeed the series plays with that by having Shinji remark that she is "like a mother".

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 29, 2019 1:32 pm Rei is the name Yui and Gendo would have given their child if it was female, so that's probably where "Rei is Shinji's sister" comes from. (Mostly. I showed NGE to someone with zero foreknowledge and they guessed immediately that Rei was Shinji's sister.)

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Melkor » Fri Nov 29, 2019 2:30 pm Being that Rei is a clone of Yui and shares most of her DNA, wouldn't that make her Yui's sort-of twin sister instead? So in a sense, Rei is actually Shinji's aunt.

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by MirrorUniverseAsuka » Sun Dec 15, 2019 11:43 pm I think you're missing a few 'great's, matey.

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 18, 2019 9:14 pm To add to this bit: Reichu wrote: Rei is the name Yui and Gendo would have given their child if it was female, so that's probably where "Rei is Shinji's sister" comes from.

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Cowboy Unit-03 » Fri Jan 03, 2020 5:05 am Rei is essentially his sister, seeing how theyre related socially and genetically. (though they arent exactly close, Rei is a peer to him) in the literal sense though, reis a clone of his mom i guess it an interpretation thing, and seeing how things are, a fair interpretation at that

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Jan 05, 2020 7:49 pm Cowboy Unit-03 wrote: Rei is essentially his sister, seeing how theyre related socially and genetically.

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 05, 2020 8:11 pm Rei isn't part Lilith on a genetic level. She's always described as a "copy" of Yui. It's similar language as what's used to describe the Evas' relationship with Adam.

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Jan 05, 2020 10:00 pm Reichu wrote: Rei isn't part Lilith on a genetic level.

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 05, 2020 10:55 pm Rei's body is probably made from Lilith's, meaning she would be genetically a Lilin but compositionally the same as Evas, Seeds, and Adam's Children. These are all made from some sort of exotic "high-energy" variety of matter, which logically would lend itself to the resident soul being able to generate a more powerful ATF in turn. A possibly useful observation here is that, when Lilith takes on Rei's form, the bridge describes the resultant being as being both pattern blue and being "human" (as opposed to "Angel", and therefore, in this context, meaning "Lilin" specifically). Compare to Kaworu, who despite his Lilin appearance resulted in a Magi analysis of "Pattern Blue" and "Angel", despite actually being at least part Lilin genetically. This discrepancy doesn't make much sense to me unless the Magi are set to seek at least some "Angel DNA" in the target, and designate any match (which it hasn't already been told to ignore on account of being an Eva) as "Angel". Kaworu, due to one of his parents being Adam, would have satisfied this parameter; Lilith-Rei, assuming she reproduced Rei's body to the cellular level, would not, because Rei's body is just a Yui clone and has no "Angel DNA".

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Jan 06, 2020 9:27 pm Reichu wrote: Rei's body is probably made from Lilith's, meaning she would be genetically a Lilin but compositionally the same as Evas, Seeds, and Adam's Children. These are all made from some sort of exotic "high-energy" variety of matter, which logically would lend itself to the resident soul being able to generate a more powerful ATF in turn.

[]: Re: Rei is Shinji's sister (...not!)             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Mar 04, 2020 3:06 pm ^This. Lillith would register as more human like. To me the whole point of Rei's character has always been inextricably tied to her ultimately being her own person regardless of the 'source material' (which seems to be backed by the recently translated EVA 2 material)and to equate her with either is to reduce all her nuanced detailed characterization, struggles and relationships sprinkled in over all the episodes to just that reveal You might say that she's got no relatives at all since she was artificially created, there's the angel component etc. and even if she was a 100% clone of Yui that would make her more like a twin so like a long lost aunt than a sister if you wanted to be hung up on the technicalities. But in the end human relationships aren't fully defined or dictated by those. One thing that really bothers me about this is how "sister" just gets throws around as a label meaning "person you like but don't want to fuck" like noo a sibling bond is actually a specific type of relationship . To all you only children, the closest thing would be a childhood friend. Like it's based specifically on how you've seen this person pooping in their pyjamas or trying to snatch your toys etc. so there's no real distance and you know all the embarassing secrets and you've got insider jokes... this is really not the case with Shinji and Rei at all they never once lived together, he never uses her first name. Like ppl just slap that label on there to tell the world that they most definitely don't ship it, but it's not there in the text. Maybe they could potentially slip into that sort of dynamic sometime in the future, perhaps as a reaction to the reveal. But as to what's actually there during the timeframe of the show and movies - they're co-workers, classmates, special friends that played some pivotal role in a part of their lives, certainly comrades. Human relationships really have a lot more dimension than just the "want to fuck/don't want to fuck" dichotomy. The closest thing to a sibling-like element there would really be through Gendo who is the closest thing Rei has to a parental figure, so there's that shared bond a bit of a lowkey rivalry and a complex slightly different relation with each of the two etc. He probably thinks of them as his kids, but that's only semi tangential to how they think of each other (or, indeed, of him.)
